summary: Steward timeout: 11 review suggestion(s) for triage
detail: [{"severity": "suggestion", "location": "dashboard/tests/test_burndown_data.py:243-245", "category": "clarity_of_design_intent", "description": "The test now has a visually asymmetric assertion block: line 243 uses `str(base_config.project_root)` (trusting the __post_init__ invariant) while lines 244-245 explicitly call `.resolve()` on `reify_root` and `autopilot_root`. These are orchestrator-discovered paths that go through `burndown.py:96`'s point-of-use `.resolve()`, not config paths, so the asymmetry is architecturally correct \u2014 but there is no comment explaining the distinction. A future maintainer could easily interpret the explicit `.resolve()` calls as an oversight and remove them, silently breaking the test on systems where tmp_path is a symlink.", "suggested_fix": "Add a brief inline comment: `assert str(reify_root.resolve()) in project_ids  # orchestrator-discovered path: resolved at burndown.py:96, not via __post_init__`", "reviewer": "reviewer_architect_reviewer"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "dashboard/src/dashboard/data/burndown.py:96", "category": "implicit_two-tier_resolution_model", "description": "The diff correctly leaves the `.resolve()` call at line 96 in place for orchestrator-discovered paths, but the refactor now creates an implicit two-tier resolution model with no explicit documentation at the call site: (1) config paths resolved via `DashboardConfig.__post_init__`, (2) orchestrator-discovered paths resolved ad-hoc at point of use. The comment added on lines 103-104 for `known_project_roots` documents tier-1. A parallel comment at line 96 would make the two-tier model explicit and prevent a future reader from assuming this call is redundant (since config paths no longer need it) and incorrectly removing it.", "suggested_fix": "Add a comment above line 96: `# project_root comes from orchestrator process inspection, not from config \u2014 resolve here`", "reviewer": "reviewer_architect_reviewer"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "dashboard/src/dashboard/config.py:28-37", "category": "eager_io_on_construction", "description": "Path.resolve() issues stat/readlink syscalls for every DashboardConfig construction. Moving resolution from lazy call sites into __post_init__ means every dataclasses.replace(), test fixture, and library import that constructs a config now pays the I/O cost eagerly, even when the resolved paths are never used. For a config object constructed once at startup this is negligible, but if configs are cloned frequently via dataclasses.replace() (e.g., per-request overrides or test parametrization), the cumulative syscall count grows unnecessarily.", "suggested_fix": "This is acceptable given configs are constructed infrequently. If hot-path cloning becomes a concern, cache a frozen resolved-flag and skip re-resolution in replace() copies (requires a custom __copy__ or sentinel field). Alternatively, document that callers should avoid replace() on Path fields in tight loops.", "reviewer": "reviewer_performance"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "dashboard/tests/test_burndown_data.py:243-244", "category": "inconsistent_resolve_calls", "description": "Two test assertions still call .resolve() on local test variables (reify_root.resolve(), autopilot_root.resolve()) that were created via tmp_path and are already canonical. These are harmless but add unnecessary syscalls in the test suite and are inconsistent with the intent of the refactor (centralize resolution into the config).", "suggested_fix": "Since tmp_path directories are real paths without symlinks, use str(reify_root) and str(autopilot_root) directly, matching the pattern used elsewhere in the updated tests.", "reviewer": "reviewer_performance"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "dashboard/src/dashboard/data/burndown.py:96", "category": "redundant_normalization", "description": "The diff cleans up `.resolve()` calls on config paths but leaves a redundant `.resolve()` on orchestrator-discovered roots at line 96: `root_str = str(project_root.resolve())`. The two code paths that produce `project_root` here are `_resolve_project_root()` and `_read_project_root_from_config()` \u2014 both already call `.resolve()` internally before returning. This `.resolve()` call is now the only non-config site that still does manual resolution after the cleanup, making the pattern inconsistent across the function.", "suggested_fix": "Change `root_str = str(project_root.resolve())` to `root_str = str(project_root)` and add a comment like the one added for `known_root` above, e.g. `# project_root is already resolved by _resolve_project_root / _read_project_root_from_config`.", "reviewer": "reviewer_reuse_auditor"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "dashboard/tests/test_burndown_data.py:244-245", "category": "inconsistent_cleanup", "description": "The diff updates `str(base_config.project_root.resolve())` \u2192 `str(base_config.project_root)` in the same test method but leaves `str(reify_root.resolve())` and `str(autopilot_root.resolve())` unchanged on the following two assertion lines. Both `reify_root` and `autopilot_root` are passed to `DashboardConfig` as `known_project_roots`, so `__post_init__` already resolves them; the stored project_ids in the DB come from the resolved values. The `.resolve()` calls are harmless on non-symlinked `tmp_path` dirs, but the partial cleanup leaves the test inconsistent and could mislead future readers into thinking these paths need manual resolution.", "suggested_fix": "Change `assert str(reify_root.resolve()) in project_ids` \u2192 `assert str(reify_root) in project_ids` and similarly for `autopilot_root`, matching the pattern applied to `base_config.project_root` in the same diff. Also update the `_tasks_map` keys on lines 225-226 and the query parameter on line 341 for the same reason.", "reviewer": "reviewer_reuse_auditor"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "dashboard/tests/test_burndown_data.py:225-226,244-245", "category": "test_consistency", "description": "In `test_snapshots_known_project_roots_when_no_orchestrators`, the `_tasks_map` still builds keys with `reify_root.resolve()` and `autopilot_root.resolve()`, and the project-id assertions on lines 244-245 also call `.resolve()`. Meanwhile line 243 correctly uses `str(base_config.project_root)` with no `.resolve()`. Since these paths are stored in the config after `__post_init__` resolution, the `.resolve()` calls are redundant but the inconsistency within the same test is misleading and will confuse anyone adding a symlink-based variant of this test in future \u2014 they might incorrectly assume `.resolve()` is still required in some paths.", "suggested_fix": "Use `str(reify_root)` and `str(autopilot_root)` in the assertions (lines 244-245) to be consistent with line 243. For the `_tasks_map` keys, also drop the `.resolve()` calls since the config will store the resolved path and that is what `collect_snapshot` will pass to `load_task_tree`.", "reviewer": "reviewer_test_analyst"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "dashboard/tests/test_scaffold.py:100-112", "category": "coverage_gap", "description": "There is a test (`test_from_env_resolves_known_project_roots`) that covers symlink resolution of `DASHBOARD_KNOWN_PROJECT_ROOTS` via `from_env()`, but no equivalent test covers `DASHBOARD_PROJECT_ROOT` pointing to a symlink. This is a new guarantee introduced by moving resolution into `__post_init__` \u2014 before this diff, `from_env()` never resolved `project_root`. The docstring in `__post_init__` explicitly calls out `from_env()` as a covered path, so the gap in coverage is against a stated design invariant.", "suggested_fix": "Add a test alongside `test_from_env_resolves_known_project_roots`: create a real dir and a symlink, set `DASHBOARD_PROJECT_ROOT` to the symlink path via `monkeypatch.setenv`, call `DashboardConfig.from_env()`, and assert `cfg.project_root == real_dir.resolve()`.", "reviewer": "reviewer_test_analyst"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "dashboard/tests/test_scaffold.py:258-304", "category": "coverage_gap", "description": "The `TestPostInit` docstring explicitly lists `dataclass.replace()` as a construction path that must produce resolved configs, but there is no test for it. While Python's `dataclasses.replace()` calls `__init__` (and therefore `__post_init__`), the explicit claim in the docstring makes this a stated design contract. If someone later wraps construction or switches to a different copy mechanism (e.g., manual `__init__` bypass, `copy.copy`, or attrs-style `evolve()`), there is no test to catch the regression.", "suggested_fix": "Add a test to `TestPostInit`: create a base config with a real dir, then call `dataclasses.replace(cfg, known_project_roots=[symlink])` and assert the resulting config has resolved `known_project_roots`. This both validates the claim and serves as a canary if the construction mechanism changes.", "reviewer": "reviewer_test_analyst"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "dashboard/src/dashboard/config.py:36-37", "category": "silent_degradation", "description": "Path.resolve() silently degrades when a path does not exist at construction time. In Python 3.6+, resolve() on a non-existent path does NOT raise FileNotFoundError \u2014 it returns the absolute form but cannot follow symlinks for non-existent path components. The docstring promise that 'consumers never need to call .resolve() on config paths' silently breaks if project_root or any known_project_roots path does not exist on disk when DashboardConfig is instantiated (e.g. in tests using tmp_path before directories are created, or during early startup before mounts are ready). The resolved path may look absolute but still contain unresolved symlink segments.", "suggested_fix": "Either (a) document the limitation explicitly ('canonical only for paths that exist at construction time'), or (b) add a validation step that warns or raises if a path does not exist: `if not self.project_root.exists(): logger.warning('project_root %s does not exist at config construction time; symlinks may not be fully resolved', self.project_root)`.", "reviewer": "reviewer_robustness"}, {"severity": "suggestion", "location": "dashboard/src/dashboard/data/burndown.py:96", "category": "toctou_asymmetry", "description": "Orchestrator-discovered paths are resolved freshly at every collect_snapshot invocation (line 96: `str(project_root.resolve())`), while config.project_root and known_project_roots are resolved once at DashboardConfig construction time. If a symlink target changes between config instantiation and a collection run, orchestrator paths will resolve to the new target but known_project_roots will still carry the stale target. This asymmetry can cause the `seen_roots` deduplication set to miss a match for a path that is logically the same project, resulting in double-counting a project in the snapshot.", "suggested_fix": "Resolve the orchestrator path against the same epoch by either (a) also storing orchestrator paths without a fresh .resolve() (trusting that _resolve_project_root/_read_project_root_from_config already returns canonical paths), or (b) accepting the asymmetry and documenting it, since symlink retargeting mid-run is an extremely unlikely operational scenario.", "reviewer": "reviewer_robustness"}]
